title: Melicope coodeana
intro: Melicope coodeana, also known as the Coode's melicope, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to New Caledonia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Melicope coodeana include limonoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Melicope coodeana is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications for skin and wound care.
taxonomy: Melicope coodeana is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Melicope. The genus Melicope is a group of flowering plants endemic to Hawaii and parts of the Pacific. Melicope coodeana is further categorized as a part of the order Sapindales, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Melicope coodeana is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies that have confirmed its relationships within the Rutaceae family.
